verses.text: God addresses those who swear by His name 'but not in truth'; He foretold former things, and they came to pass. Their religion was outward. God's fulfilled predictions expose hollow profession.
God addresses those who swear by His name 'but not in truth'; He foretold former things, and they came to pass. Their religion was outward. God's fulfilled predictions expose hollow profession.
God addresses those who swear by His name 'but not in truth'; He foretold former things, and they came to pass. Their religion was outward. God's fulfilled predictions expose hollow profession.
Because He knew they were obstinate, He declared things beforehand, lest they credit their idols. He forestalls their excuses. God proves His word so men cannot give the glory to false gods.
Because He knew they were obstinate, He declared things beforehand, lest they credit their idols. He forestalls their excuses. God proves His word so men cannot give the glory to false gods.
Because He knew they were obstinate, He declared things beforehand, lest they credit their idols. He forestalls their excuses. God proves His word so men cannot give the glory to false gods.
These new things are revealed now, for He knew them treacherous, 'a transgressor from the womb.' Their sin is deep-rooted. God deals with a people whose rebellion runs from their very beginning.
These new things are revealed now, for He knew them treacherous, 'a transgressor from the womb.' Their sin is deep-rooted. God deals with a people whose rebellion runs from their very beginning.

'For my name's sake will I defer mine anger'; He has refined them 'in the furnace of affliction... for mine own sake.' Mercy spares them for His glory. God forgives and purifies His people that His name be honoured.

'For my name's sake will I defer mine anger'; He has refined them 'in the furnace of affliction... for mine own sake.' Mercy spares them for His glory. God forgives and purifies His people that His name be honoured.

'For my name's sake will I defer mine anger'; He has refined them 'in the furnace of affliction... for mine own sake.' Mercy spares them for His glory. God forgives and purifies His people that His name be honoured.

'I am the first, I also am the last'; His hand laid earth's foundations, and He has called Cyrus to prosper His way. He alone declares and performs. The Creator who spoke the world directs its history.

'I am the first, I also am the last'; His hand laid earth's foundations, and He has called Cyrus to prosper His way. He alone declares and performs. The Creator who spoke the world directs its history.

He bids them draw near; 'the Lord GOD, and his Spirit, hath sent me,' the LORD who 'teacheth thee to profit,' wishing they had heeded His commandments. He longs for their good. Had they obeyed, their peace would have flowed like a river.

He calls them to flee Babylon with singing, for the LORD has redeemed Jacob; yet 'there is no peace... unto the wicked.' Redemption and warning together. The freed rejoice, but the unrepentant find no rest.
